title: Preview Environments (GitHub + DigitalOcean)
description: Spin up a live preview environment for every pull request. Comment /start on a PR, get a running app in ~2 minutes using DigitalOcean Droplets.
requirements: SSH key added to both DigitalOcean and SuperPlane
A setup script at scripts/preview-setup.sh in your repo
agentInstructions: The user just installed the Preview Environments app. This canvas creates ephemeral preview environments for pull requests using GitHub and DigitalOcean. Setup usually involves: 1) Connecting their GitHub integration (needs Deployments permission), 2) Connecting their DigitalOcean integration, 3) Adding an SSH key as a secret, 4) Updating the repository name and setup script URL in the canvas nodes to match their project.
agentInitialMessage:
This canvas is a fully wired preview environment system for GitHub pull requests using DigitalOcean Droplets. It is ready structurally, but it still points at template defaults, so connect your own [GitHub](integration:github) and [DigitalOcean](integration:digitalocean) integrations before running it.

## What the canvas does

PR open flow:
- [PR Opened](node:pr-opened) posts [Welcome Comment](node:welcome-comment) with `/start` and `/destroy` instructions.

Preview deploy flow:
- [/start](node:deploy-cmd) reacts to the PR comment, [Ack Deploy](node:ack-deploy) marks it seen, and [Check Exists](node:check-exists) looks in `preview-envs` memory.
- If no environment exists, [Create Deployment](node:create-deployment) creates a GitHub Deployment, [Status: In Progress](node:deploy-status-progress) marks it `in_progress`, [Create Droplet](node:create-droplet) provisions the droplet, and [Setup App](node:setup-app) runs the setup script over SSH.
- On success, [Save Environment](node:save-env) records the preview, [Health Check](node:http-health-check) tests it, [Status: Success](node:deploy-status-success) marks the deployment successful, and [Post Preview URL](node:comment-ready) comments the URL.
- If setup fails, [Status: Failure](node:deploy-status-failure) reports the failed deployment, [Setup Failed](node:setup-failed-comment) comments the error, and [Cleanup Failed Droplet](node:cleanup-failed-droplet) removes the droplet.
- If the health check fails after setup, [Health Check Warning](node:health-failed-comment) still shares the preview URL with a warning.

Destroy and cleanup flows:
- [/destroy](node:destroy-cmd) starts manual cleanup: [Read Env](node:read-env-destroy), [Delete Droplet](node:delete-droplet-cmd), [Cleanup Memory](node:cleanup-cmd), [Status: Inactive](node:deploy-status-inactive-cmd), and [Destroyed Comment](node:comment-destroyed-cmd).
- [PR Closed](node:pr-closed) runs the close cleanup path with [Read Env (Close)](node:read-env-close), [Delete Droplet (Close)](node:delete-droplet-close), [Cleanup Memory (Close)](node:cleanup-close), and [Destroyed Comment (Close)](node:comment-destroyed-close).
- [TTL Check](node:ttl-schedule) runs hourly, [Read All Envs](node:read-all-envs) loads previews, [Older than 24h?](node:check-ttl) filters expired records, [TTL Delete](node:ttl-delete-droplet) deletes droplets, [Status: Inactive (TTL)](node:ttl-deploy-status-inactive) closes deployments, [TTL Cleanup](node:ttl-cleanup) clears memory, and [TTL Expired Comment](node:ttl-expired-comment) comments on the PR.

## Setup checklist

| Task | Current state |
|---|---|
| Connect [GitHub](integration:github) | Required for [PR Opened](node:pr-opened), [/start](node:deploy-cmd), [/destroy](node:destroy-cmd), deployment status nodes, and comment nodes |
| Connect [DigitalOcean](integration:digitalocean) | Required for [Create Droplet](node:create-droplet), [Delete Droplet](node:delete-droplet-cmd), [Delete Droplet (Close)](node:delete-droplet-close), [TTL Delete](node:ttl-delete-droplet), and [Cleanup Failed Droplet](node:cleanup-failed-droplet) |
| Add SSH private key secret | [Setup App](node:setup-app) expects `preview-digital-ocean-ssh-key` with key `private_key` |
| Update repository name | GitHub nodes and memory tags currently use `example-app` / `example/example` |
| Update setup script URL | [Setup App](node:setup-app) currently downloads `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/example/example/main/scripts/preview-setup.sh` |
| Update DigitalOcean SSH key | [Create Droplet](node:create-droplet) currently uses `preview-digital-ocean-ssh-key` |

## GitHub setup

Connect [GitHub](integration:github) with Deployments permission. Standard PR/Issues permissions are not enough because [Create Deployment](node:create-deployment), [Status: In Progress](node:deploy-status-progress), [Status: Success](node:deploy-status-success), [Status: Failure](node:deploy-status-failure), and the inactive status nodes all use GitHub Deployments.

## DigitalOcean setup

Connect [DigitalOcean](integration:digitalocean), then update the droplet nodes: [Create Droplet](node:create-droplet), [Delete Droplet](node:delete-droplet-cmd), [Delete Droplet (Close)](node:delete-droplet-close), [TTL Delete](node:ttl-delete-droplet), and [Cleanup Failed Droplet](node:cleanup-failed-droplet). The template creates Ubuntu 24.04 droplets in `nyc1` using size `s-1vcpu-1gb`, named `example-app-pr-<PR number>`, and tagged with `example-app` and `preview`.

## SSH and app setup

[Setup App](node:setup-app) logs in as `root` and expects a SuperPlane secret named `preview-digital-ocean-ssh-key` with key `private_key`. It downloads and runs:

`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/example/example/main/scripts/preview-setup.sh`

Create that secret in SuperPlane and update the setup script URL to your own repository. The script should install dependencies, start the app, and serve it on the droplet so [Health Check](node:http-health-check) can verify `http://<droplet-ip>.sslip.io`.

Connected integrations in use:
- [GitHub](integration:github)
- [DigitalOcean](integration:digitalocean)

title: Preview Environments (GitHub + AWS)
description: Spin up a live preview environment for every pull request. Comment /start on a PR, get a running app in ~2 minutes using AWS EC2 instances.
requirements: SSH key added to both AWS and SuperPlane
A setup script at scripts/preview-setup.sh in your repo
agentInstructions: The user just installed the Preview Environments app. This canvas creates ephemeral preview environments for pull requests using GitHub and AWS. Setup usually involves: 1) Connecting their GitHub integration (needs Deployments permission), 2) Connecting their AWS integration, 3) Adding an SSH key as a secret, 4) Updating the repository name and setup script URL in the canvas nodes to match their project.
agentInitialMessage:
This canvas is a fully wired preview environment system for GitHub pull requests using AWS EC2. It is ready structurally, but it still points at template defaults, so connect your own [GitHub](integration:github) and [AWS](integration:aws) integrations before running it.

## What the canvas does

PR open flow:
- [PR Opened](node:pr-opened) posts [Welcome Comment](node:welcome-comment) with `/start` and `/destroy` instructions.

Preview deploy flow:
- [/start](node:deploy-cmd) reacts to the PR comment, [Ack Deploy](node:ack-deploy) marks it seen, and [Check Exists](node:check-exists) looks in `preview-envs` memory.
- If no environment exists, [Create Deployment](node:create-deployment) creates a GitHub Deployment, [Status: In Progress](node:deploy-status-progress) marks it `in_progress`, [Create Instance](node:create-droplet) provisions EC2, and [Setup App](node:setup-app) runs the setup script over SSH.
- On success, [Save Environment](node:save-env) records the preview, [Health Check](node:http-health-check) tests it, [Status: Success](node:deploy-status-success) marks the deployment successful, and [Post Preview URL](node:comment-ready) comments the URL.
- If setup fails, [Status: Failure](node:deploy-status-failure) reports the failed deployment, [Setup Failed](node:setup-failed-comment) comments the error, and [Cleanup Failed Instance](node:cleanup-failed-droplet) terminates the instance.
- If the health check fails after setup, [Health Check Warning](node:health-failed-comment) still shares the preview URL with a warning.

Destroy and cleanup flows:
- [/destroy](node:destroy-cmd) starts manual cleanup: [Read Env](node:read-env-destroy), [Delete Instance](node:delete-droplet-cmd), [Cleanup Memory](node:cleanup-cmd), [Status: Inactive](node:deploy-status-inactive-cmd), and [Destroyed Comment](node:comment-destroyed-cmd).
- [PR Closed](node:pr-closed) runs the close cleanup path with [Read Env (Close)](node:read-env-close), [Delete Instance (Close)](node:delete-droplet-close), [Cleanup Memory (Close)](node:cleanup-close), and [Destroyed Comment (Close)](node:comment-destroyed-close).
- [TTL Check](node:ttl-schedule) runs hourly, [Read All Envs](node:read-all-envs) loads previews, [Older than 24h?](node:check-ttl) filters expired records, [TTL Delete Instance](node:ttl-delete-droplet) terminates instances, [Status: Inactive (TTL)](node:ttl-deploy-status-inactive) closes deployments, [TTL Cleanup](node:ttl-cleanup) clears memory, and [TTL Expired Comment](node:ttl-expired-comment) comments on the PR.

## Setup checklist

| Task | Current state |
|---|---|
| Connect [GitHub](integration:github) | Required for [PR Opened](node:pr-opened), [/start](node:deploy-cmd), [/destroy](node:destroy-cmd), deployment status nodes, and comment nodes |
| Connect [AWS](integration:aws) | Required for [Create Instance](node:create-droplet), [Delete Instance](node:delete-droplet-cmd), [Delete Instance (Close)](node:delete-droplet-close), [TTL Delete Instance](node:ttl-delete-droplet), and [Cleanup Failed Instance](node:cleanup-failed-droplet) |
| Add SSH private key secret | [Setup App](node:setup-app) expects `preview-aws-ssh-key` with key `private-key` |
| Update repository name | GitHub nodes and memory tags currently use `example-app` / `example/example` |
| Update setup script URL | [Setup App](node:setup-app) currently downloads `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/example/example/main/scripts/preview-setup.sh` |
| Update AWS EC2 settings | [Create Instance](node:create-droplet) currently uses `us-east-1`, `t3.micro`, AMI `ami-02fd066b86800f60c`, key pair `preview-aws-ssh-key`, subnet `subnet-0fe49d94faf9cac72` |

## GitHub setup

Connect [GitHub](integration:github) with Deployments permission. Standard PR/Issues permissions are not enough because [Create Deployment](node:create-deployment), [Status: In Progress](node:deploy-status-progress), [Status: Success](node:deploy-status-success), [Status: Failure](node:deploy-status-failure), and the inactive status nodes all use GitHub Deployments.

## AWS setup

Connect [AWS](integration:aws), then update the EC2 nodes: [Create Instance](node:create-droplet), [Delete Instance](node:delete-droplet-cmd), [Delete Instance (Close)](node:delete-droplet-close), [TTL Delete Instance](node:ttl-delete-droplet), and [Cleanup Failed Instance](node:cleanup-failed-droplet). The template creates Ubuntu EC2 instances in `us-east-1` using `t3.micro`, assigns a public IP, and configures security group access for HTTP, HTTPS, and SSH.

## SSH and app setup

[Setup App](node:setup-app) logs in as `ubuntu` and expects a SuperPlane secret named `preview-aws-ssh-key` with key `private-key`. It downloads and runs:

`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/example/example/main/scripts/preview-setup.sh`

Create that secret in SuperPlane and update the setup script URL to your own repository. The script should install dependencies, start the app, and serve it on the instance so [Health Check](node:http-health-check) can verify `http://<instance-ip>.sslip.io`.

Connected integrations in use:
- [GitHub](integration:github)
- [AWS](integration:aws)
